name: Serra de São Paulo Trail (PR1)
name_original: Percurso pela Serra de São Paulo (PR 1) —Serra de São Mamede
description: Hike the moderate 6.6-mile Tour through Serra de São Paulo (PR 1) in Serra de São Mamede, enjoying diverse landscapes and views.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Santa Maria Da Devesa traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, natural parks, and tranquil reservoirs. The region offers a mix of rural paths and trails within the Serra de São Mamede Nature Park. These routes often feature gravel roads and natural surfaces, providing accessible outdoor experiences. Best easy hiking trails around Santa Maria Da Devesa The most popular easy hiking route is Apartadura Reservoir – Ti Esperança Spring loop from Serra de São Mamede Nature Park, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 43 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding nature. Another top favourite among local hikers is Beirã – Gravel road loop from Beirã, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This route guides hikers through rural landscapes on well-maintained gravel roads. Local hikers also love the Apartadura Reservoir – Albufeira Reservoir loop from Serra de São Mamede Nature Park, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through the natural park and past two reservoirs, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around Santa Maria Da Devesa is defined by reservoir paths, natural park trails, and rural gravel roads. The network offers numerous easy hiking trails suitable for various fitness levels. The routes in Santa Maria Da Devesa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 290 reviews. Nearly 1,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Maria Da Devesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Santa Maria Da Devesa?
A: There are 16 easy hiking trails around Santa Maria Da Devesa, offering a variety of landscapes from reservoir paths to rural gravel roads.

Q: Are there circular easy walks in Santa Maria Da Devesa?
A: Yes, many easy trails in the region are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31683174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Apartadura Reservoir – Ti Esperança Spring loop from Serra de São Mamede Nature Park&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14705747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beirã – Gravel road loop from Beirã&lt;/a&gt; are both excellent circular options.

Q: What kind of attractions can I expect to see on easy hikes?
A: Easy hikes often lead through the Serra de São Mamede Nature Park and past tranquil reservoirs. You can also find historical sites nearby, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/659213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Village of Marvão&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/659211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;16th century bridge of Portagem&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails suitable for families?
A: Yes, the easy hiking trails are generally suitable for families. They feature well-maintained gravel roads and natural surfaces with minimal elevation gain, making them accessible for various ages and fitness levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32134377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portagem River Beach – Roman City of Ammaia loop from Marvão&lt;/a&gt; is a good option for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails?
A: Many trails in the Santa Maria Da Devesa region are dog-friendly, especially those on rural paths and gravel roads. Always ensure your dog is under control and check for any specific local regulations within the Serra de São Mamede Nature Park.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Maria Da Devesa?
A: The region is pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter offers cooler, often crisp conditions, ideal for those who prefer a quieter experience.

Q: What should I wear for easy hikes in this area?
A: For easy hikes, comfortable walking shoes or light hiking boots are recommended. Dress in layers to adapt to changing temperatures, and consider bringing a hat, sunscreen, and sufficient water, especially during warmer months.

Q: Where can I find parking for easy hikes?
A: Parking is typically available near trailheads, especially in villages like Beirã or at popular starting points within the Serra de São Mamede Nature Park. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages on komoot.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Maria Da Devesa?
A: The trails are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 290 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil reservoir views, the well-maintained gravel roads, and the peaceful rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical castles or settlements to explore near the easy trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marvão Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/401156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Vide&lt;/a&gt;, both of which are accessible and offer a glimpse into the area's past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32134377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portagem River Beach – Roman City of Ammaia loop from Marvão&lt;/a&gt; even passes by ancient Roman ruins.

Q: Do the easy hikes offer good viewpoints?
A: Many easy hikes provide scenic views, particularly those that traverse the rolling hills or pass by the reservoirs. The routes within the Serra de São Mamede Nature Park often open up to panoramic vistas of the surrounding natural landscape.
tips: Magnificent viewpoint over Castelo de Vide.

Built before 1570, at the height of the Portuguese Renaissance, the chapel of Nossa Senhora da Penha stands tall at the top of the Serra de São Paulo, at an altitude of 710 metres. To get there, you have to climb a steep staircase, an effort rewarded by the serenity of the place and the breathtaking views.

Although the original image of Senhora da Penha was stolen, a replica now occupies its place on the altar.

From the top, the view is breathtaking: the town stretches out below, with its watchful castle, white houses and rolling fields; to the east, the imposing quartzite ridge of Marvão stands out; in the background, the River Sever marks the border, and to the north the horizon is bordered by the Açor, Gardunha and Estrela mountains.

Main square of Castelo de Vide. 

"Urban centre and municipal headquarters. Town situated on a hill on the border of Alentejo. Medieval town under the jurisdiction of a religious-military order (Order of Christ) with castle and urban enclosure. 17th-century war square integrated into the defence line of the Alentejo border." 
in http://www.monumentos.gov.pt/Site/APP_PagesUser/SIPA.aspx?id=10463

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Tour through Serra de São Paulo (PR 1) and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This hike is classified as moderate. While it requires a good level of fitness, the paths are generally easily accessible, making it a manageable option for most skill levels, including those with some hiking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park, featuring mountainous terrain, sloping heathlands, rocky ridges, and forests. You'll walk on paths characterized by a mix of granite, shale, and limestone outcrops. Despite the rugged environment, the route is known for its easily-accessible paths.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: As you hike, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the Geodesic Marker of Facho – View of Castelo de Vide, offering expansive vistas. Another highlight is the View of Castelo de Vide from Senhora da Penha. The route also passes through Dom Pedro V Square (Castelo de Vide), providing a glimpse into the local settlement.

faqs.question: Does the Tour through Serra de São Paulo (PR 1) intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with other official trails within the park. You may find yourself on parts of the Circuito de Manutenção, Calçada Medieval, and Caminho dos Marrões during your hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this route. The weather is typically milder, and the natural park's lush greenery and diverse flora are at their most vibrant. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Tour through Serra de São Paulo (PR 1) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please be mindful of any specific signage along the trail regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit to hike the Tour through Serra de São Paulo (PR 1), and there is no entrance fee to access the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park. The park is open to the public for exploration.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this hike?
faqs.answer: Specific trailhead parking information is not detailed, but given its proximity to Castelo de Vide and the nature of PR trails, it's likely you can find parking in or near the town. Look for designated parking areas close to the start of the marked PR1 route.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plant life can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Serra de São Mamede Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, including Bonelli's eagles, vultures, and other eagles. The park is also home to over 800 plant species, with lush greenery, oak, chestnut, and cork oak forests dominating the landscape. Keep an eye out for otters and, if you're very lucky, even the elusive Iberian lynx.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The 'Tour through Serra de São Paulo (PR 1)' is designed as a circular route, meaning it's a loop trail. You'll start and end at roughly the same point, offering a continuous journey through the landscape without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate hike like this?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing appropriate hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also advisable, though the trail is generally well-marked.
